name: Startup Act Labellisation
description: Official merit label providing startups access to finance, tax benefits, and international exposure with funds ranging 30k–200k TND
eligibility_criteria: Innovative business model with differentiated solution
More than 2/3 capital held by individuals or regulated investment organizations
Tunisian nationality or registered in Tunisia
notes: Over 1,450 startups labeled as of 2026; application processed via online portal startup.gov.tn

name: Flat6Labs Tunis Seed Program
description: 4-month accelerator providing seed funding, strategic mentorship, office space, and business training for early-stage startups
eligibility_criteria: Team must dedicate fully to 4-month program
Startup at early stage with prototype or MVP
Based in Tunisia or willing to relocate
notes: Largest accelerator in the region; launched in Tunisia 2016; provides cash funding upon selection

name: Wiki Startup Incubator
description: First private incubator/accelerator in Tunisia (created 2011) supporting entrepreneurs, investors, and public/private entities
notes: University incubator; collaborates with EPT for EPT Startup Incubator hub

name: Orange Fab Tunisie
description: 100% free 3–6 month acceleration program creating commercial partnerships between startups and Orange Business Units
eligibility_criteria: Respond to need of Orange Business Unit (100%)
Service commercialized or commercializable in Tunisia or elsewhere (50%)
notes: Part of Orange Fab network; first call launched Oct–Nov 2024; access to international market via Orange Group

name: B@Labs BIAT
description: BIAT-supported incubation platform offering free non-equity support covering pre-incubation, incubation, and post-incubation cycle
notes: Launched 2017; non-profit; Avenue Habib Bourguiba, Tunis; part of Fondation BIAT pour la Jeunesse

name: BFPME Création de PME
description: Public bank financing for SME creation with investment cost 100k–10M TND; co-financing only unless company 3+ years old
eligibility_criteria: Total investment cost between 100,000 DT and 10 million DT
notes: Repayment: 2–10 years (grace period up to 2 years); created March 2005; 34 Rue Hédi Karray, Centre Urbain Nord

name: BTS Crédit Solidaire
description: Tunisian Solidarity Bank loans for SME creation up to 300,000 TND at 5% annual rate, 7-year repayment with 1-year grace
notes: Grants ~15,000 loans/year with 300M TND budget; applications via updated BTS website platform; launched communitarian companies financing 2023

name: Business Builder Challenge
description: Fully digital program offering tailored accompaniment and funding up to $50,000 (~150,000 TND) for Tunisian entrepreneurs
notes: Deadline June 30, 2025; fully digital; ~150,000 TND maximum funding

name: UNDP timbuktoo Initiative
description: Pan-African UNDP initiative unlocking innovation potential through tailored funding, expert mentorship, and global market access for high-potential startups
notes: Co-designed with 500 Global; includes Pre-Acceleration Academy (6–12 Oct), Sustainable Innovation Seed Accelerator

name: GIZ Digital Transformation Center Tunisia
description: GIZ project supporting Tunisian ICT sector with training, job creation, start-up support, Industry 4.0, and Startup Act implementation
notes: Supporting MTCTD since 2019; develops digital centre; 50+ strategic partnerships with European companies targeted

name: CAWTAR–GIZ Catalyser l'entrepreneuriat inclusif
description: Joint CAWTAR–GIZ program with 150,000 EUR budget supporting 120+ entrepreneurial structures (70% women beneficiaries)
notes: Program concluded May 2026 after 1 year; 6 best initiatives received 5,000 dinars each individually

name: Carthage Business Angels
description: First angel investors network in Tunisia, not-for-profit 3E Agency (Entrepreneurial Ecosystem Enabler)
notes: Active in African and Latin American markets; ecosystem enabler role

name: CNBees
description: First crowdfunding platform specifically for Social and Solidarity Economy in Tunisia
notes: Launched December 2024; key milestone for local support

name: Cha9a9a.tn
description: Associative online donation platform enabling associations and individuals to create donation campaigns for solidarity/humanitarian causes
notes: 100% secure and easy to use; associative platform

name: AFD Tunisia Private Sector Facility
description: French Development Agency loans 500k–5M EUR for SMEs plus Entrepreneurship Support Program grants up to 100k EUR
notes: Multiple financing instruments; technical assistance programs included

name: EIB JEREMIE Tunisia
description: European Investment Bank 50M EUR guarantee fund for SMEs via JEREMIE program
notes: Partnership with BFPME for SME lending; InnovFin up to 2M EUR for innovative startups also available

name: IFC Tunisia SME Investment Loan
description: International Finance Corporation investment loans up to 10M TND for SMEs including working capital and long-term financing
notes: Private sector focus; investment loans for manufacturing and services

name: RNE Registre National des Entreprises
description: National business registry where all Tunisian companies must register; 5–7 day duration requiring business form, tax ID, and legal documents
notes: Digital registration available via portal; approximately 5–7 days processing time

name: CNSS Registration M1 Form
description: CNSS employee and entrepreneur registration required within 30 days of business creation via online portal with M1 form
notes: 30-day deadline; online registration; M1 form mandatory

name: APII Innovation Grant
description: APII innovation grants up to 50,000 TND for SMEs with technical support and Industrie 4.0 digital transformation program
notes: Industrie 4.0 program for digital transformation of manufacturing companies; industrial zoning permits also available

name: INPQ ISO Certification Framework
description: Tunisian National Organization for Standardization and Quality provides ISO 9001 (quality) and ISO 14001 (environmental) certification for export
notes: ISO 9001 for quality management; ISO 14001 for environmental management; export-focused

name: CTC Chambre de Commerce Business Guide
description: CTC provides business registration guides, export documentation assistance, trade fairs participation, legal advisory, and regular training sessions
eligibility_criteria: Membership required for certain export procedures
notes: Chambre de Commerce et d'Industrie de Tunisie; regular training sessions on business management

name: ETZI Industrie Guide
description: Sector-specific guide for manufacturing industry in Tunisia covering regulations, standards, and export requirements
notes: Confidence: low — URL or criteria unverified; needs manual check

name: Nuqta Co-working Space
description: Co-working space for entrepreneurs and self-employed workers helping startups develop with community and social network
notes: Confidence: low; one of 152 coworking spaces in Tunisia as of May 2025

name: Westerwelle Startup Haus Tunis
description: Global foundation coworking space in Tunis supporting startups with focus environment, networking, and community
notes: Global foundation; launched October 2024; 500 sq meters integrated coworking

name: EPT Startup Incubator
description: École Polytechnique de Tunisie and Wiki Startup joint hub introducing students to entrepreneurship with workspace, mentorship, expertise, and investor access
notes: First private incubator in Tunisia partnership; 4 Rue Abou Zakaria El Hafsi, Montfleury, Tunis

name: Essect Ensit Business Incubator (E²BI)
description: University business incubator at 4 Rue Abou Zakaria El Hafsi providing entrepreneurship support
notes: E²BI; 1089 Montfleury, Tunis; fax +216 71 333 518

name: Start-up Business Accelerator
description: Business accelerator supporting startups with acceleration programs and investor connections
notes: Confidence: low; URL or criteria unverified — needs manual check

name: Start-up Space By Enpact Tunisia
description: Enpact Tunisia startup space providing national mentoring and entrepreneurship support
notes: National mentoring program; Enpact organization

name: The Next Society
description: Company supporting entrepreneurs with welcome services and ecosystem connections
notes: Confidence: low; URL or criteria unverified — needs manual check

name: FLYWHEEL Financial Support Program
description: Financial support program developed in Tunisian startup ecosystem for startup funding
notes: One of several vehicles developed alongside Startup Act; active ecosystem program

name: Halo Business Angel Activation
description: Business Angel activation program connecting startups with angel investors in Tunisian ecosystem
notes: Part of ecosystem programs; 9 active Business Angel Networks in Tunisia

name: Invest'i Investor Matching
description: Investor matching program connecting startups with appropriate investors in Tunisian ecosystem
notes: 500+ investment operators in ecosystem; active investor matching program

name: Horizon Europe Tunisia Participation
description: EU research and innovation funding with Tunisian participants securing €15.5M across 64 grants and 86 participations
notes: Tunisia ranks 12th among associated countries; strongest Southern Mediterranean in PRIMA with 198 participations; food systems, water management, agro-environmental focus

name: ENI CBC Italy-Tunisia Programme
description: Italy-Tunisia ENI CBC Programme 2014-2020 with 27 projects worth €30M supporting SMEs, research, innovation, and climate adaptation
notes: Closure event December 2024; Sicily Region organized; EU Regional Development Fund funded

name: UNDP Acceleration Pilot Program 2024
description: UNDP program for AI startups addressing SDGs with partnership building, capability enhancement, and sustainable development drive
notes: Deadline August 5, 2024; 9 pilot countries including Tunisia; AI Hub for Sustainable Development pillars

name: Women-only MSME Financing Line
description: Women-only financing line with favorable credit conditions through BTS, BNA, and BFPME banks
notes: Three banks: BTS, BNA, BFPME; 800,000+ beneficiaries in 2024; TEG 25.7% at end 2024

name: Tunisia Microfinance Institutions (7 IMF)
description: Seven microfinance institutions with 232 filiales across Tunisia providing financing up to 40,000 TND for 7 years
notes: 232 filiales; 8th associative institution; 289 microfinance associations; ACM controls sector

name: GGJAP Green Growth Jobs Accelerator
description: Danish-Arab Partnership Program-funded regional initiative by Flat6Labs partnership with UNDP for green growth and jobs
notes: Regional initiative; implemented by Flat6Labs with UNDP partnership; green growth focus

name: BFPME Medium-Long Term Credit
description: Medium-long term credits for PME, startups, and agri/pêche companies for investment and exploitation needs
notes: Article 85 of 2026 Finance Law activated; three partnership conventions signed April 2023; 2024-2028 business plan

name: BFPME Women Financing Line
description: Women-specific financing line through BFPME with favorable credit conditions
notes: Part of women-only line with BTS and BNA; favorable conditions

name: Startup Ecosystem Fund
description: Startup Act Startup Ecosystem framework funds ranging 30k–200k TND with training and international exposure
notes: $9,855–$65,703 USD equivalent; supports conception through growth; startup ecosystem and startup invest frameworks

name: Startup Invest VC Framework
description: Startup Act investment framework creating high-add-value VC fund ecosystem with investment guarantees and financial advice
notes: Pool investor funds; provide investment guarantees; national/international expert training programs

name: ANETI Self-Employment Program
description: National employment agency self-employment program supporting entrepreneurs with job creation and business formation
notes: ANETI — National Employment Agency; self-employment and entrepreneurship focus

name: BTS Communitarian Companies Financing
description: BTS financing for communitarian companies up to 300,000 TND at 5% for 7 years with 1-year grace; 15 companies funded 4M TND in 2023
notes: 100 communitarian companies to be created 2024; first batch 7 companies 1.6M TND end 2023; Finance Law 2023 line

name: CoFundy Diaspora Crowdfunding
description: Donation-based crowdfunding platform by Tunisian diaspora (Thameur Hemdane, Amina Nasri) for Maghreb startups
notes: Founded by Tunisians in France; Afrikwity equity platform also by same founders; 2016 launch

name: Afrikwity Equity Platform
description: Equity investment platform allowing investments in return for equity stakes in Maghreb/African startups
notes: By same founders as CoFundy; equity stakes return model

name: PRIMA Agro-Environmental Research
description: EU PRIMA program where Tunisia leads with 198 participations in food systems, water management, agro-environmental research
notes: Tunisia strongest Southern Mediterranean country; 198 participations; leadership in food systems, water, agro-environment

name: Tech Export Ecosystem
description: Growing tech export ecosystem built through Startup Act with 1,450+ labeled startups and tech export focus
notes: Eighth anniversary 2026; 1,450+ startups labeled; growing tech export ecosystem
